draft.introduction: The Visuddhimagga by Buddhaghosa is the supreme compendium of Theravāda Buddhist practice. It maps the entire path through three stages: virtue, concentration, and wisdom.

translation.text: When a wise man, established well in virtue, develops consciousness and understanding, then as a monk ardent and sagacious, he succeeds in disentangling this tangle. Herein, “virtue” is the beginning of the path.
translation.original_text: sīle patiṭṭhāya naro sapanno | cittaṃ pannancha bhāvayam

translation.text: There are seven stages of purification: purification of virtue, purification of mind, purification of view, purification by overcoming doubt, purification by knowledge and vision of what is and is not the path, purification by knowledge and vision of the way, and purification by knowledge and vision.

translator_notes.note: The Visuddhimagga’s seven-stage purification scheme derives from the Rathavinīta Sutta (MN 24). Buddhaghosa’s contribution is encyclopedic elaboration: each stage receives detailed practical instruction, making it the most comprehensive meditation manual in any tradition.
